message:
test(resolv): POSIX/hosts(5) conformance tables for /etc/hosts (bd-x7jd)

Add two spec-derived conformance tables (Pattern 4) citing the clause
that motivates each expected outcome:

hosts_parse_conformance_table (16 cases):
  • HOSTS-PARSE-00x  whitespace (space / tab / mixed runs)
  • HOSTS-PARSE-01x  address families (IPv4 dotted quad, IPv6 loopback
                     and full literal)
  • HOSTS-PARSE-02x  comments and blank lines
  • HOSTS-PARSE-03x  RFC 952 / RFC 1123 hostname character set
                     (leading digit, interior hyphen, multi-alias)
  • HOSTS-PARSE-04x  malformed input rejection (non-IP first field,
                     name-less IP, out-of-range octet)

hosts_lookup_conformance_table (7 cases):
  • HOSTS-LOOKUP-001/002  RFC 1035 §2.3.3 case insensitivity (both
                          sides)
  • HOSTS-LOOKUP-003      alias entries match query
  • HOSTS-LOOKUP-004      multiple lines → multiple addresses
  • HOSTS-LOOKUP-005      IPv4/IPv6 mixed for same name
  • HOSTS-LOOKUP-006      comment lines are skipped
  • HOSTS-LOOKUP-007      no-match returns empty set

Each case includes spec_ref citing hosts(5) / RFC 952 / RFC 1123 /
RFC 1035. Complements existing unit tests by tying each observable
behavior to a specific spec clause for audit purposes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "bd-kan", "title": "EPIC: Math Kernel Rebuild (production vs research; alien artifact but relevant)", "description": "Critique mapping: #5.

Goal: remove the ‘ornamental math’ smell while preserving true alien-artifact quality.

Deliverables:
- Relevance audit: every runtime_math module must justify itself with:
  - which runtime decision it drives
  - invariants + evidence ledger
  - measured benefit vs a simpler baseline
- Split membrane into production core vs opt-in research annex.

Acceptance:
- Default build includes only math that is actively used and measurably beneficial.
- Hot path remains within strict/hardened perf budgets.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.", "notes": "## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "assignee": "AzureBluff", "created_at": "Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T22:25:05Z"}, {"id": 1103, "issue_id": "bd-kan", "author": "AzureBluff", "text": "Closure proposal evidence (AzureBluff, 2026-02-19): bd-kan objective is now satisfied by completed governance/value-proof/partition chain.

Closed blocker chain now in place:
- bd-24x: relevance ledger (decision linkage mapping)
- bd-2yx: production vs research governance classification
- bd-3tp: value-proof A/B evidence
- bd-545: retirement gate policy
- bd-rqn: production manifest partition + compile-time feature routing baseline
- bd-3ot + bd-3ot.1/.2/.3: admission policy + controller manifest + CI enforcement

Current runtime-math production state:
- production manifest is minimal and explicit:
  - production_modules=25
  - research_only_modules=44
- admission and retirement gates pass with deterministic artifacts/logs.
- controller manifest artifact exists and is machine-checkable.

Validation recap:
- scripts/check_runtime_math_manifest.sh PASS
- scripts/check_runtime_math_classification_matrix.sh PASS
- scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh PASS
- scripts/check_math_retirement.sh PASS
- scripts/check_runtime_math_admission.sh PASS
- scripts/check_math_governance.sh PASS
- rch harness tests for admission/classification/policy/retirement all PASS.

Given the above, this epic’s stated deliverable (“remove ornamental math smell while preserving explainable production core and research annex”) is met.

 {"id": "bd-kcbm", "title": "C.2: Wire gshadow parser to getsgent/getsgnam/fgetsgent/sgetsgent ABI exports", "description": "## Goal
Replace the 8 gshadow stub functions in pwd_abi.rs (lines 963-1040) with real implementations that read /etc/gshadow using the core parser from bd-ta3b (C.1).

## Functions to Implement (8 total)

### Non-reentrant (4 functions, use static thread-local buffer)
1. getsgent() -> *mut sgrp: Sequential enumeration of /etc/gshadow entries. Uses thread-local state tracking current line offset. Returns pointer to static sgrp struct.
2. getsgnam(name) -> *mut sgrp: Lookup by group name. Read /etc/gshadow, find matching entry.
3. fgetsgent(stream) -> *mut sgrp: Read one line from a FILE* stream, parse as gshadow.
4. sgetsgent(string) -> *mut sgrp: Parse a single gshadow-format C string.

### Reentrant _r variants (4 functions, write into caller buffer)
5. getsgent_r(result_buf, buffer, buflen, result) -> c_int
6. getsgnam_r(name, result_buf, buffer, buflen, result) -> c_int
7. fgetsgent_r(stream, result_buf, buffer, buflen, result) -> c_int
8. sgetsgent_r(string, result_buf, buffer, buflen, result) -> c_int

Return 0 on success, ENOENT if not found, ERANGE if buffer too small.

### Also update existing stubs
- setsgent(): Reset enumeration state to beginning of file
- endsgent(): Clear enumeration state and close any cached fd

## struct sgrp Layout (must match glibc <gshadow.h>)
```c
struct sgrp {
    char  *sg_namp;    // group name
    char  *sg_passwd;  // encrypted password
    char **sg_adm;     // NULL-terminated array of administrator names
    char **sg_mem;     // NULL-terminated array of member names
};
```
Size: 32 bytes on x86_64 (4 pointers).

## Implementation Details

### File reading
Read /etc/gshadow via SYS_openat + SYS_read into a stack buffer. Cache content for getsgent enumeration. Same pattern as existing getpwent/getgrent implementations.

### Static buffer for non-_r variants
Thread-local static buffer: sgrp struct + string data + pointer arrays.
```rust
thread_local! {
    static GSHADOW_BUF: RefCell<Vec<u8>> = RefCell::new(vec![0u8; 4096]);
    static GSHADOW_SGRP: Cell<sgrp> = Cell::new(zeroed_sgrp());
}
```

### Buffer packing for _r variants
Pack into caller buffer: name string + NUL, password string + NUL, admin pointers array + NUL ptr, member pointers array + NUL ptr, admin strings + NULs, member strings + NULs. Return ERANGE if total exceeds buflen.

### Handling missing /etc/gshadow
If /etc/gshadow doesn't exist (common on many systems), all functions return null/ENOENT gracefully. This is correct behavior — glibc does the same.

### Comma-separated list splitting
The sg_adm and sg_mem fields in struct sgrp are char** (NULL-terminated pointer arrays). The raw gshadow line has comma-separated names. We must split on commas and create an array of C string pointers within the buffer.

## Unit Tests (in tests/pwd_abi_test.rs)
- test getsgnam_root: getsgnam("root") returns non-null on systems with /etc/gshadow, or null gracefully if file missing
- test getsgnam_nonexistent: getsgnam("zzz_no_such_group_zzz") returns null
- test getsgnam_null_name: getsgnam(NULL) returns null (no crash)
- test getsgent_enumerates: call getsgent repeatedly until null, verify count > 0 (on systems with /etc/gshadow)
- test setsgent_resets_enumeration: setsgent, getsgent, setsgent, getsgent returns same first entry
- test sgetsgent_parses_line: sgetsgent("test:x:admin1:mem1,mem2") returns correct struct
- test sgetsgent_null_returns_null: sgetsgent(NULL) returns null
- test getsgnam_r_erange: getsgnam_r with buflen=1 returns ERANGE
- test getsgnam_r_success: getsgnam_r with large buffer returns 0 and valid struct
- test fgetsgent_null_stream: fgetsgent(NULL) returns null

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_gshadow_native.sh
1. Check if /etc/gshadow exists (skip gracefully if not)
2. Build and run: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pwd_abi_test gshadow
3. If /etc/gshadow exists: also run under LD_PRELOAD to verify getsgnam works
4. Emit structured JSONL report

## Structured Logging
Each function emits: trace_id, api_family=pwd, symbol=getsgnam|getsgent|..., name (if applicable), result_status, errno, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 8 functions parse /etc/gshadow correctly when it exists
- Graceful null/ENOENT return when /etc/gshadow missing
- _r variants handle ERANGE correctly
- 10+ unit tests pass
- e2e script passes
- cargo clippy clean", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:18:54.549518127Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T02:52:57.629640629Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T02:52:57.629254598Z", "close_reason": "Wired gshadow parser to all 8 ABI exports. getsgnam/getsgent read /etc/gshadow via core parser. sgetsgent parses C strings. Non-_r variants use thread-local static buffer. _r variants pack into caller buffer with ERANGE on overflow. pack_gshadow_into_buf handles comma-split admin/member pointer arrays. setsgent/endsgent manage enumeration offset. fgetsgent stays as null-return (safe - can't read arbitrary FILE* in interpose model). 34 pwd_abi tests + 14 core gshadow tests pass. Clippy clean.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["abi", "pwd", "stub-elimination"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-kcbm", "depends_on_id": "bd-ta3b", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:19:02.378470572Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

Add differential testing for string manipulation (wcsncpy, wcscat, wcsncat, wcschr, wcsrchr, wcsstr, wcstok, wcsspn, wcscspn, wcspbrk) and memory ops (wmemcpy, wmemmove, wmemset, wmemcmp, wmemchr).","status":"completed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:36:18.505600516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:42:05.219472361Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Requirements:

- Mapping from runtime events -> byte payload.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CobaltForge", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:34:56.179506346Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T15:59:32.230342536Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T15:59:32.230323721Z", "close_reason": "done (record format + ring buffer + payload mapping implemented; see evidence.rs + format doc)", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-kom", "depends_on_id": "bd-3a9", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1104, "issue_id": "bd-kom", "author": "GrayPond", "text": "Implemented bd-kom EvidenceSymbolRecord v1 + systematic ring buffer and payload mapping.

Delivered:

- Byte-level format spec + payload schema (EVR1/EVP1): crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence_symbol_record_format.md
- Overwrite-on-full publication semantics + snapshot protocol; per-(mode,family) epoch stream state + hash chaining: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs
- Module wired into runtime_math namespace: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs

Notes:
- Repair-symbol generation is explicitly deferred to bd-1es (cadence-only) and integration to bd-3ku.
- All work is merged in commit 864395b.", "created_at": "2026-02-10T15:59:29Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-kx74", "title": "EPIC: Membrane activation and runtime policy hardening", "description": "The Transparent Safety Membrane (TSM) validation pipeline is currently in permanent passthrough mode under LD_PRELOAD. signal_runtime_ready() was removed from startup because activating the membrane caused hangs and crashes due to re-entrancy in the validation pipeline.

CURRENT STATE:
- runtime_policy::decide() always returns passthrough (RUNTIME_READY is never set to 1)
- All ABI functions run without membrane validation
- The membrane code exists and works in tests, but is disabled at runtime

ROOT CAUSE: The ValidationPipeline uses RwLock internally. When an interposed function (e.g., malloc) goes through the pipeline, and the pipeline calls a function that itself is interposed (causing re-entry), the RwLock deadlocks.

SOLUTION APPROACH:
1. Make the pipeline re-entrant (use try_read instead of read, fast-path on contention)
2. OR: Only activate membrane after all bootstrap is complete and all interposed functions have host delegation fallbacks
3. OR: Use a per-thread re-entrancy flag to skip validation when already inside the pipeline

This is the next major milestone after the current LD_PRELOAD compatibility work.

RATIONALE: The TSM is the core innovation of FrankenLibC. Without it active, we're just a passthrough libc wrapper. Activating it is essential for the project's value proposition.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:06:54.923380535Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-02T19:48:49.201570576Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-02T19:48:49.201289334Z", "close_reason": "All children closed: bd-kx74.1 (per-thread reentracy guard) and bd-kx74.2 (signal_runtime_ready re-enabled). Membrane activation and runtime policy hardening complete.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "bd-kx74.1", "title": "Implement per-thread re-entrancy guard for membrane validation pipeline", "description": "Add a thread-local boolean flag MEMBRANE_REENTRY that skips validation when a thread is already inside the pipeline. This prevents the RwLock deadlock that caused signal_runtime_ready to be removed.

APPROACH:
1. Add thread_local! { static MEMBRANE_ACTIVE: Cell<bool> = Cell::new(false); }
2. In runtime_policy::decide(), check MEMBRANE_ACTIVE first — if true, return passthrough
3. Set MEMBRANE_ACTIVE to true before entering the pipeline, false after
4. This allows nested calls (malloc inside validation) to bypass the pipeline

RISK: Some functions SHOULD be validated even during nested calls. The per-thread flag is a coarse solution. A more nuanced approach would be per-API-family re-entrancy tracking.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:06:55.120006400Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:46:37.715103006Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:46:37.714070612Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-kx74.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-kx74", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:06:55.120006400Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "bd-x2sq", "title": "bd-1rf subtask: NSS strict+hardened fixture/e2e closure with matrix drift checks", "description": "Background:
- NSS backend completion must be reflected in conformance/e2e gates and support matrix truth.

Goal:
- Build strict/hardened NSS fixture pack and close evidence loop for supported files backend APIs.

Deliverables:
1) Fixture sets for passwd/group/hosts lookups.
2) E2E scenarios using integration binaries.
3) Support matrix and reality report consistency updates.

Acceptance Criteria:
- NSS fixture/e2e suite passes deterministically in target scope.
- Docs/matrix/report remain drift-free under guard scripts.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E scripts with comprehensive structured logs and artifact refs.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "2026-02-18 status (IcyCat): NSS fixture executor wiring completed in crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs; NSS fixture packs expanded (pwd_ops/grp_ops/resolver); new integration fixture tests/integration/fixture_nss.c added and c_fixture_spec/link_test updated. Verification: cargo fmt --check PASS; rch cargo check/clippy PASS; rch cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance PASS (57 tests); rch bash scripts/check_c_fixture_suite.sh PASS.  {"id": "bd-xxd9", "title": "bd-25n subtask: Threading/futex lifecycle exhaustive unit+stress test packs", "description": "Background:
- Threading and synchronization logic remains a critical defect surface.

Goal:
- Build exhaustive unit test packs for pthread/futex/thread bootstrap lifecycle semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Mutex/condvar/thread/join/detach API-level unit tests.
2) Concurrency adversarial tests with deterministic scheduling controls where feasible.
3) Regression suite for known deadlock/hang signatures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Threading test packs cover success, misuse, contention, timeout, teardown paths.
- Regression signatures are pinned and automatically checked.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit and stress test scripts.
- Structured logs with thread/event timelines and failure markers.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "DustyRiver", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:02:38.991066758Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-16T14:34:54.992244359Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-16T14:34:54.992224261Z", "close_reason": "Thread/futex lifecycle pack now includes mutex/condvar/rwlock/once/tsd + lifecycle regressions (including self-join). Validated via targeted rch test binaries and workspace quality gates.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "pthread", "testing", "unit", "verification"], "comments": [{"id": 1217, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9", "author": "DustyRiver", "text": "Taking ownership of `bd-xxd9` based on `bv` robot triage.

Execution start:

  - `python3_print` timeout

", "created_at": "2026-02-16T06:19:58Z"}, {"id": 1218, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Progress update for `bd-xxd9` (threading/futex lifecycle unit+stress packs):

 {"id": "bd-xxd9.1", "title": "Native pthread self-join can deadlock under thread lifecycle path", "description": "Observed while developing bd-xxd9 regression tests: a child thread invoking pthread_join on its own pthread_t can hang indefinitely instead of returning EDEADLK. Repro path came from an ABI test that passed self handle via shared slot and called pthread_join(self). This can wedge the lifecycle suite (global test guard then blocks subsequent tests). Need root-cause fix in native join/create lifecycle path plus deterministic non-hanging regression.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "DustyRiver", "created_at": "2026-02-16T07:04:33.822441437Z", "created_by": "DustyRiver", "updated_at": "2026-02-16T14:31:41.100312883Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-16T14:31:41.100295009Z", "close_reason": "Self-join deadlock hardened: core+ABI guards in place; regression test re-enabled in default lifecycle suite and validated via repeated rch runs + workspace gates.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["pthread", "regression", "threading"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-xxd9.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-xxd9", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1223, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9.1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "`bd-xxd9.1` investigation update (self-join deadlock):

Repro status:
- Repro remains valid: explicit self-join regression (`pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk`) can hang >60s when run standalone.
- This is why the self-join regression remains excluded from default suite (no default CI wedging).

What I tested:
1. Parent-side `tid` publication in `create_thread` (store returned child tid before startup wait).
2. Child-side early `tid` publication in trampoline.
3. Removed `std::thread::yield_now()` from helper (to avoid clone/TLS interaction suspicion) in explicit repro path.

Outcome:
- None of the above alone eliminated the explicit self-join hang in this environment.
- Default lifecycle suite remains stable with stress tests opt-in/ignored.

Current safe posture:
- Keep self-join issue tracked as `bd-xxd9.1` (in_progress).
- Keep non-hanging lifecycle/condvar regressions active in default runs.
- Keep stress/endurance paths opt-in (`#[ignore]`) until self-join path is resolved.

Validation re-confirmed during this pass:
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`6 passed, 5 ignored`)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`7 passed`)
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
  (all run through `rch exec -- ...`; artifact retrieval warnings from worker are environmental and occurred after successful remote command exit)
", "created_at": "2026-02-16T07:24:14Z"}, {"id": 1224, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9.1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Progress update (self-join hardening pass):

Implemented:
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs`: added `ThreadHandle.self_tid` (stable child-captured identity), early publication of child tid/self_tid in trampoline, and an additional running-state self-join guard in `join_thread` before futex wait paths.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs`: added ABI-level early self-join rejection in `native_pthread_join`:
  - direct `thread == my_tid` guard for fallback raw-TID self IDs
  - registry-backed check against target handle `tid/self_tid` while `THREAD_STARTING|THREAD_RUNNING` before handle removal/core join call.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`: kept `pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk` as explicit opt-in (`#[ignore]`) due remaining scheduler-sensitive startup window during this bead; avoids default-suite wedge while hardening continues.

Validation (using rch for cargo test/check/clippy):
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`6 passed, 6 ignored`)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`7 passed`)
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅

Status:
- Deadlock incidence reduced in explicit probes, but startup-order nondeterminism still appears in repeated forced self-join probes; keeping this bug in-progress for another pass to make self-join assertion fully deterministic and unignore regression.
", "created_at": "2026-02-16T14:08:24Z"}, {"id": 1225, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9.1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "New execution slice started from `bv --robot-triage`:

- `bd-25n` remains highest-impact node but blocked by `bd-xxd9`.
- `bd-xxd9.1` remains the active blocker bug in this lane.

This slice focus:
1) Remove startup-order nondeterminism in self-join path (`pthread_join(pthread_self())`).
2) Drive deterministic behavior toward strict `EDEADLK` in explicit probe runs.
3) If stable, unignore `pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk`; otherwise keep it explicit opt-in with updated evidence.

Starting implementation now in:
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs`
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs`
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`
", "created_at": "2026-02-16T14:28:33Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-xxyb", "title": "Investigate Python startup perf regression in LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (51/7/6)", "description": "## Problem
LD_PRELOAD smoke suite regressed from 58/0/6 to 51/7/6. All 7 failures are Python-related with failure_signature=startup_perf_regression. Correctness is fine (parity_pass=true) — only startup latency exceeds the perf gate threshold.

## Failing Cases
- strict: python3_print, stress_python_{1..5}
- hardened: stress_python_5

## Probable Cause
Recent changes to signal handling and malloc that added overhead:
1. signal_abi.rs: Signal trampoline installation, SA_RESTORER marshalling, deferred delivery critical sections
2. malloc_abi.rs: Signal critical section entry/exit around every malloc/calloc/free/realloc call
3. host_resolve.rs: Streaming /proc/self/maps parser changes

Python startup is particularly sensitive because it calls sigaction dozens of times during interpreter init, and does heavy allocation.

## Investigation Steps
1. Profile Python startup under LD_PRELOAD to identify the hotspot
2. Check if the malloc signal critical section is the bottleneck (atomic ops on every alloc)
3. Check if sigaction trampoline installation overhead is significant
4. Consider: make signal critical sections opt-in (only when FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened)

## Acceptance Criteria
Smoke suite returns to 58/0/6 (or 57/0/7 with the new skipped binary). Python startup perf within threshold.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-03T18:43:55.100768393Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-04T00:24:27.961421919Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-04T00:23:57.951709506Z", "close_reason": "Smoke gate back to 58/0/6; strict+hardened Python cases green", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["malloc", "performance", "regression", "signal"], "comments": [{"id": 1367, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "ubuntu", "text": "Investigation finding: strace -c output is garbled under LD_PRELOAD because our printf engine does NOT support POSIX positional format specifiers (%n$s, %n$d). strace uses these extensively for column-aligned output. This is a separate correctness bug from the perf regression. The perf regression itself appears to be from the 275ms Python startup (vs 15ms native) — likely caused by signal trampoline overhead per sigaction call (67 calls) and /proc/self/maps reads (6 reads). The positional printf bug should be a separate bead.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T18:46:53Z"}, {"id": 1368, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "ubuntu", "text": "Root cause analysis: The 275ms Python startup (vs 15ms native) is primarily from host symbol resolution overhead during early bootstrap, not from the signal trampoline per se. Each of the 67 sigaction calls triggers runtime_policy::decide() + kernel struct marshalling + trampoline installation, but the main cost is likely the resolve_host_symbol_raw calls reading /proc/self/maps (6 reads confirmed via strace). The signal trampoline itself is cheap after first install. Possible fixes: (1) cache /proc/self/maps content, (2) lazy-resolve symbols on first actual use not on every sigaction, (3) batch symbol resolution at startup. This is an optimization task, not a correctness bug.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T18:47:54Z"}, {"id": 1374, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "LavenderMarsh", "text": "First perf datapoint after strict allocator fast-path reorder in malloc_abi.rs: rebuilt release lib via rch (cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --release) and remeasured local python3 -c print(1) startup over 5 runs. Baseline avg ~16.99 ms, strict avg ~61.11 ms, down from the previous ~290.72 ms on the pre-patch release artifact. This strongly suggests the strict-mode startup regression was paying allocator signal/policy scaffolding on the host path. Awaiting the post-patch hardened measurement before deciding whether sigaction needs a separate fast path for bd-xxyb.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T20:12:57Z"}, {"id": 1375, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "LavenderMarsh", "text": "Second perf fix in progress: added a strict-mode fast path in signal_abi::sigaction so strict mode skips runtime_policy::decide/observe entirely and goes straight to validation + rt_sigaction marshalling, since strict mode never repairs signal installs. This targets the remaining Python startup tax after the allocator host-path reorder.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T20:14:40Z"}, {"id": 1376, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "LavenderMarsh", "text": "Fixed strict-mode Python startup regression in two steps. 1) host-thread pthread/TLS split in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs: host-created threads now use host pthread_self, pthread_key_{create,delete}, and pthread_{get,set}specific; fresh strict preload dropped gettid calls from ~2252 to 2. 2) strict-mode raw fast paths for memcpy/strlen in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs so hot string ops skip runtime_policy::decide in strict mode; fresh local 5-run sample against /tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so improved strict startup from ~58 ms to ~37 ms. Current strict syscall profile: gettid=2, clock_gettime=142, futex=135. Verification via rch: cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed; cargo check --workspace --all-targets passed; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; cargo fmt --check clean.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T20:32:05Z"}, {"id": 1377, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "LavenderMarsh", "text": "Closure evidence: rebuilt fresh release artifact via rch into /data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so, then ran the real smoke gate with an explicit pinned lib path after fixing scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh to honor FRANKENLIBC_SMOKE_LIB_PATH. Command: rch exec -- env FRANKENLIBC_SMOKE_LIB_PATH=/data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target TIMEOUT_SECONDS=10 STRESS_ITERS=5 bash scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh. Result: Summary passes=58 fails=0 skips=6, artifact bundle at target/ld_preload_smoke/20260404T002003Z. All strict and hardened Python cases passed, including python3_print and stress_python_{1..5}. This satisfies bd-xxyb acceptance criteria.", "created_at": "2026-04-04T00:21:07Z"}]}

@@ -857,4 +857,277 @@ mod tests {
         let err = getnameinfo(&addr, 0).unwrap_err();
         assert_eq!(err, EAI_FAMILY);
     }
+
+    // -----------------------------------------------------------------
+    // hosts(5) / RFC 952 / RFC 1123 conformance table (bd-x7jd)
+    // -----------------------------------------------------------------
+    //
+    // Spec sources:
+    //   • hosts(5) — /etc/hosts format and parsing semantics
+    //   • RFC 952 — original hostname grammar
+    //   • RFC 1123 §2.1 — hostname relaxation (digits as first char)
+    //
+    // Each entry cites the clause that motivates the expected parse
+    // outcome. Reviewers can audit behavior by cross-referencing.
+
+    struct HostsParseCase {
+        id: &'static str,
+        spec_ref: &'static str,
+        input: &'static [u8],
+        expected_addr: Option<&'static [u8]>,
+        expected_names: &'static [&'static [u8]],
+    }
+
+    const HOSTS_PARSE_CONFORMANCE_TABLE: &[HostsParseCase] = &[
+        // ---- Whitespace handling (hosts(5) "whitespace") ----
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-001",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶Description — single space separator",
+            input: b"127.0.0.1 localhost",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"127.0.0.1"),
+            expected_names: &[b"localhost"],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-002",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶Description — tab separator",
+            input: b"127.0.0.1\tlocalhost",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"127.0.0.1"),
+            expected_names: &[b"localhost"],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-003",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶Description — mixed runs of spaces/tabs",
+            input: b"127.0.0.1 \t  localhost\t \tloopback",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"127.0.0.1"),
+            expected_names: &[b"localhost", b"loopback"],
+        },
+        // ---- Address families ----
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-010",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶IP_address — IPv4 dotted quad",
+            input: b"192.0.2.1 example",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"192.0.2.1"),
+            expected_names: &[b"example"],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-011",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶IP_address — IPv6 loopback",
+            input: b"::1 localhost6 ip6-loopback",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"::1"),
+            expected_names: &[b"localhost6", b"ip6-loopback"],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-012",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶IP_address — full IPv6 literal",
+            input: b"2001:db8::1 ipv6host",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"2001:db8::1"),
+            expected_names: &[b"ipv6host"],
+        },
+        // ---- Comments & blank lines ----
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-020",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶Description — '#' begins comment",
+            input: b"# pure comment line",
+            expected_addr: None,
+            expected_names: &[],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-021",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶Description — '#' terminates parseable content",
+            input: b"10.0.0.1 host # trailing comment",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"10.0.0.1"),
+            expected_names: &[b"host"],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-022",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶Description — blank line is ignored",
+            input: b"",
+            expected_addr: None,
+            expected_names: &[],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-023",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶Description — whitespace-only line is blank",
+            input: b"   \t\t   ",
+            expected_addr: None,
+            expected_names: &[],
+        },
+        // ---- RFC 952 / RFC 1123 hostname character sets ----
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-030",
+            spec_ref: "RFC 1123 §2.1 — digits permitted as first char",
+            input: b"10.0.0.5 3com.local",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"10.0.0.5"),
+            expected_names: &[b"3com.local"],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-031",
+            spec_ref: "RFC 952 — hyphens allowed interior",
+            input: b"10.0.0.6 my-host-name",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"10.0.0.6"),
+            expected_names: &[b"my-host-name"],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-032",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶alias — multiple aliases share a line",
+            input: b"10.0.0.7 primary alias1 alias2 alias3",
+            expected_addr: Some(b"10.0.0.7"),
+            expected_names: &[b"primary", b"alias1", b"alias2", b"alias3"],
+        },
+        // ---- Malformed input rejection ----
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-040",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶IP_address — non-IP first field rejected",
+            input: b"not-an-ip hostname",
+            expected_addr: None,
+            expected_names: &[],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-041",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶Description — IP without any name is rejected",
+            input: b"127.0.0.1",
+            expected_addr: None,
+            expected_names: &[],
+        },
+        HostsParseCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-PARSE-042",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) ¶IP_address — IPv4 octet > 255 rejected",
+            input: b"999.0.0.1 bad",
+            expected_addr: None,
+            expected_names: &[],
+        },
+    ];
+
+    #[test]
+    fn hosts_parse_conformance_table() {
+        let mut fails = Vec::new();
+        for case in HOSTS_PARSE_CONFORMANCE_TABLE {
+            let actual = parse_hosts_line(case.input);
+            let pass = match (&actual, case.expected_addr) {
+                (None, None) => true,
+                (Some((addr, names)), Some(expected_addr)) => {
+                    addr == expected_addr
+                        && names.len() == case.expected_names.len()
+                        && names
+                            .iter()
+                            .zip(case.expected_names.iter())
+                            .all(|(a, b)| a.as_slice() == *b)
+                }
+                _ => false,
+            };
+            if !pass {
+                fails.push(format!(
+                    "{} [{}]: expected addr={:?} names={:?}, got {:?}",
+                    case.id,
+                    case.spec_ref,
+                    case.expected_addr.map(|a| core::str::from_utf8(a).unwrap_or("<non-utf8>")),
+                    case.expected_names
+                        .iter()
+                        .map(|n| core::str::from_utf8(n).unwrap_or("<non-utf8>"))
+                        .collect::<Vec<_>>(),
+                    actual,
+                ));
+            }
+        }
+        assert!(
+            fails.is_empty(),
+            "hosts(5) parse conformance failures:\n  {}",
+            fails.join("\n  ")
+        );
+    }
+
+    // ---- lookup_hosts conformance (case-insensitivity + multi-address) ----
+
+    struct HostsLookupCase {
+        id: &'static str,
+        spec_ref: &'static str,
+        content: &'static [u8],
+        query: &'static [u8],
+        expected: &'static [&'static [u8]],
+    }
+
+    const HOSTS_LOOKUP_CONFORMANCE_TABLE: &[HostsLookupCase] = &[
+        HostsLookupCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-LOOKUP-001",
+            spec_ref: "RFC 1035 §2.3.3 — DNS is case-insensitive; hosts lookup mirrors it",
+            content: b"10.0.0.1 MyHost\n",
+            query: b"myhost",
+            expected: &[b"10.0.0.1"],
+        },
+        HostsLookupCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-LOOKUP-002",
+            spec_ref: "RFC 1035 §2.3.3 — query case is also insensitive",
+            content: b"10.0.0.2 myhost\n",
+            query: b"MYHOST",
+            expected: &[b"10.0.0.2"],
+        },
+        HostsLookupCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-LOOKUP-003",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) — alias entries match query",
+            content: b"10.0.0.7 primary alias1 alias2\n",
+            query: b"alias2",
+            expected: &[b"10.0.0.7"],
+        },
+        HostsLookupCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-LOOKUP-004",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) — multiple lines may map same name to multiple IPs",
+            content: b"10.0.0.1 web\n10.0.0.2 web\n",
+            query: b"web",
+            expected: &[b"10.0.0.1", b"10.0.0.2"],
+        },
+        HostsLookupCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-LOOKUP-005",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) — mixed IPv4/IPv6 entries for same name",
+            content: b"127.0.0.1 localhost\n::1 localhost\n",
+            query: b"localhost",
+            expected: &[b"127.0.0.1", b"::1"],
+        },
+        HostsLookupCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-LOOKUP-006",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) — comment lines are skipped during lookup",
+            content: b"# 10.0.0.1 fake\n10.0.0.2 real\n",
+            query: b"fake",
+            expected: &[],
+        },
+        HostsLookupCase {
+            id: "HOSTS-LOOKUP-007",
+            spec_ref: "hosts(5) — no match returns empty set",
+            content: b"127.0.0.1 localhost\n",
+            query: b"unknown.example",
+            expected: &[],
+        },
+    ];
+
+    #[test]
+    fn hosts_lookup_conformance_table() {
+        let mut fails = Vec::new();
+        for case in HOSTS_LOOKUP_CONFORMANCE_TABLE {
+            let actual = lookup_hosts(case.content, case.query);
+            let pass = actual.len() == case.expected.len()
+                && actual
+                    .iter()
+                    .zip(case.expected.iter())
+                    .all(|(a, b)| a.as_slice() == *b);
+            if !pass {
+                fails.push(format!(
+                    "{} [{}]: expected {:?}, got {:?}",
+                    case.id,
+                    case.spec_ref,
+                    case.expected
+                        .iter()
+                        .map(|a| core::str::from_utf8(a).unwrap_or("<non-utf8>"))
+                        .collect::<Vec<_>>(),
+                    actual
+                        .iter()
+                        .map(|a| core::str::from_utf8(a).unwrap_or("<non-utf8>"))
+                        .collect::<Vec<_>>(),
+                ));
+            }
+        }
+        assert!(
+            fails.is_empty(),
+            "hosts(5) lookup conformance failures:\n  {}",
+            fails.join("\n  ")
+        );
+    }
 }
